description: A backup integrity check just finished, and you (the backup engineer) must quickly identify which files failed verification. 1. The log of the verification run is already present at /home/user/backup/logs/rsync_2023-09-01.log 2. Scan that log and extract ONLY the absolute paths of files whose verification status is “FAILED …”. • A “FAILED” line always follows this exact structure (note the two spaces before the word FAILED): YYYY/MM/DD HH:MM:SS [PID] verify /<path> FAILED <reason> • The reason text (e.g. checksum, size, etc.) varies and must be ignored. • Ignore every other line (including those containing “OK”). 3. Write the failed paths—one per line, no extra spaces, no line numbers—to /home/user/backup/verification/failed_files_2023-09-01.txt The file MUST end with a single trailing newline (i.e., POSIX-style text file). Example of the expected file content format (EXAMPLE ONLY—do not hard-code these values): /data/foo/bar.txt /data/other/baz.dat⏎ ← one final newline after the last line The entire task can be completed with a single well-crafted grep/awk/sed (or similar) command, but use whichever standard shell utilities you prefer. When you are done, you can cat the resulting file to verify its contents.
